title: product-spec-matrix.v1 — the committed record of a product's settled acceptance criteria
description: The shape of `.engine/product-spec-matrix.json`, the derived-committed matrix product-design writes (one row per settled acceptance criterion). It is pinned here as an EXPLICIT producer/consumer contract: the product-design generator (obligation_matrix.py) produces it, and the standing conformance sweep (conformance_sweep.py, a core tool) reads it by presence WITHOUT importing the optional product-design module. Without this pinned shape, a rename of a row field on the producer side would break the consumer's read silently. A test on each side validates against this file, so the cross-module by-presence read cannot drift unnoticed. An empty `rows` array is valid and expected — a project with no settled `docs/spec/` capability has an empty matrix (a first-class MVP state).

schema_version.description: Which version of this file's shape this is. Lets a future engine upgrade recognise an older matrix and migrate it safely. Today the only valid value is 1.

source.description: Where the rows are derived from — the product's own in-repo spec tree. Today the only valid value is 'docs/spec'.

rows.description: One row per settled acceptance criterion, sorted by (doc, position) for a deterministic, byte-comparable artifact. Empty when nothing is settled.

obligation-row.description: One settled acceptance criterion: the document it came from, its position in that document's criteria table, a content digest of the criterion text (the key drift attaches to), and the criterion / how-it-is-verified / who-checks-it as written.

doc.description: The repo-relative, forward-slashed path of the `docs/spec/` document the criterion came from.

position.description: The 0-based index of the criterion's row in its document's acceptance-criteria table (its validated table position). NOT part of the row's dedup identity for the standing sweep — that is (doc, digest) — because position cascades on an insert/delete.

digest.description: The content digest of the criterion cell: sha256 over the whitespace-canonicalised criterion text. The thing that moves on a genuine re-wording, and the content-key the standing sweep attaches a finding to.

criterion.description: The acceptance criterion as written in the spec.

how_verified.description: How the criterion is verified, as written in the spec.

who.description: Who checks the criterion, as written in the spec (lowercased).
